summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #118 by enabling stdio-based MCP servers (npm packages) to run inside the Moltis Docker container, which was missing Node.js/npx.
summary.outcome: Added Node.js and npm to the Dockerfile runtime stage and documented both the built-in Node.js approach and Docker-in-Docker workaround in docs/src/mcp.md; changes were committed and pushed with a PR created.

repo: The runtime Docker image already includes docker-ce-cli and a mounted Docker socket, enabling Docker-in-Docker MCP server patterns.
The image is based on Debian bookworm, which ships Node.js 18.x via standard apt packages.
MCP server configuration lives in moltis.toml under [mcp.servers.*] sections.

code.finding: nodejs and npm were added to the runtime stage apt-get install block; the image already had chromium (~300MB) so the ~60-80MB Node.js addition was considered minor.
A new 'Running MCP Servers in Docker' section was added before 'Security Considerations', covering both direct npx usage and isolated Docker container invocation via the mounted socket.

workflow: For Docker-based MCP servers without Node.js, the docker run --rm -i pattern with npx entrypoint on node:20-alpine is a viable workaround.
Official MCP Docker images (e.g. mcp/memory) can also be used directly via the docker command in moltis.toml.

open_items: Consider using NodeSource setup script or a node:20-slim build stage if a newer Node.js version than Debian bookworm's 18.x is needed in the future.
No validation (e.g. docker build test) was run before committing — image build correctness was not verified.
